g.text: Step 1 | Append 0x80 byte immediately after last message byte
Step 2 | Append k zero bytes: k = smallest k>=0 s.t. (len+1+k) ≡ 56 (mod 64)
Step 3 | Append 8-byte big-endian encoding of (len * 8) — original bit length
Result | Total padded length is always an exact multiple of 64 bytes
55-byte msg → 1 block | 56-byte msg → 2 blocks (the classic trap)
Big-Endian Length Field — 'abc' example (len=3 bytes, bits=24=0x18)
MSB at lowest address — most significant byte 0x00 written first
0x03 | uint8_t | 0x80 marker — '1' bit + 7 zero bits — 8 bits
0x39–0x3F | uint8_t[7] | zero fill — 56 bits (NOT enough for len field)
— | (no length) | length field CANNOT fit: 7 < 8 bytes remain
Total: 64 bytes — NO length field here — SPILL to Block 1
Total: 64 bytes — length field lives here — 2-block padding
BOUNDARY RULE: if (len % 64 >= 56) → need extra block
